description: bind:map on svelte-maplibre-gl conflicts with exactOptionalPropertyTypes — type mismatch on optional Map property
resolution: Use onload={(e) => { mapInstance = e.target as unknown as MapLibreMap; }} instead of bind:map

content: Svelte 5 stores use .svelte.ts files with $state/$derived/$effect rune globals — ESLint must declare these globals for both .svelte and .svelte.ts file blocks

name: exactOptionalPropertyTypes spread
description: Use ...(val !== undefined ? { key: val } : {}) for optional spread props instead of direct assignment to satisfy exactOptionalPropertyTypes

content: Tailwind 4: @import 'tailwindcss' in app.css, @tailwindcss/vite plugin in vite.config.ts, no tailwind.config.js needed
tRPC 11 Fetch adapter at /api/trpc/[...trpc] — all API routes go through tRPC routers, not SvelteKit endpoints. Auth via Lucia v3 + @lucia-auth/adapter-drizzle with text PK sessions

description: MapLibre 5 + esbuild: class fields not transpiled correctly by default
resolution: Set build.target: 'es2022' in vite.config.ts for native class fields without helper transforms

description: ESLint OOM when parsing .svelte-kit directory or using parserOptions.project with TypeScript
resolution: Global ignores FIRST (standalone { ignores: [...] }) to prevent .svelte-kit scanning. No parserOptions.project — syntax-only rules avoid TypeScript OOM

title: superjson transformer for tRPC
rationale: tRPC serializes Date objects as ISO strings over JSON, causing type mismatches (Layer.createdAt: Date but runtime string). superjson preserves Date/Map/Set through JSON round-trips. Added to both server init.ts and client trpc.ts. Eliminates all Date-serialization casts and lets component interfaces use Date directly.
